memory:memory/2026-04-06.md:1:31.snippet: # 2026-04-06 — Daily Notes ## llama.cpp Server on llama VM (VM 199) ### CUDA Architecture Fix - **Problem:** llama-server crashing with `CUDA error: no kernel image is available for execution on the device` - **Root cause:** llama.cpp was compiled for newer CUDA arch (Ampere/Ada 8.0+) but P6000s are Pascal (CC 6.1) - **Fix:** Rebuilt with `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=61` - **Build path:** `/opt/llama.cpp/build` (owned by `llamacpp` user) - **Service:** `llama-server.service` — running, model loads and serves completions ✅ - **Endpoint:** `http://llama:11434/v1` - **GPUs:** 2x Quadro P6000 (48GB total VRAM) ### SSH Config Fix (ocg → llama) - **Problem:** `~/.ssh/config` had `User root` for

memory:memory/2026-04-06.md:26:51.snippet: - **Added:** `llama` provider in `openclaw.json` → `models.providers.llama` - **Added:** `llama/llamacpp` in `agents.defaults.models` with alias `llama` - **Config:** baseUrl `http://llama:11434/v1`, apiKey `local`, contextWindow 262144, maxTokens 8192 - **Usage:** `/model llama` or `llama/llamacpp` ## Lessons Learned - Previous model (qwen3.5) got stuck in infinite loop trying `ssh root@llama` and `process write` without sessionId - Always use `localadmin@llama` for SSH — now enforced via `~/.ssh/config` - P6000 = Pascal CC 6.1 — must compile CUDA code with `-DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=61` - Provider needs BOTH `models.providers.<name>` AND `agents.defaults.models.<provider/model>` entries

memory:memory/2026-04-06.md:45:71.snippet: - **Build path:** `/opt/llama.cpp/build` (owned by `llamacpp` user) - **Service:** `llama-server.service` — running, model loads and serves completions ✅ - **Endpoint:** `http://llama:11434/v1` - **GPUs:** 2x Quadro P6000 (48GB total VRAM) ### SSH Config Fix (ocg → llama) - **Problem:** `~/.ssh/config` had `User root` for llama host — should be `localadmin` - **Problem:** `UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null` was discarding host keys every time - **Fix:** Changed `User root` → `User localadmin`, removed `UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null` - **Result:** `ssh llama` now works correctly with key auth ### Sudoers Fix (llama) - **Problem:** NOPASSWD rule in `/etc/sudoers` was overridden by `%sudo` group ru
